reasoning: Kliment Kolesnikov is the current verified world record holder for the men's 50m backstroke in a long course (50m) pool, having set the record at 23.55 seconds on July 27, 2023. At the recent 2025 World Aquatics Championships in August, he won gold but did not break his world record, underscoring that his time remains the standard to beat. Since December through March typically lacks major long course international competitions (which generally take place in the summer, with short course events dominating the winter months), the likelihood of the long course world record being broken in the next three months is extremely low. There is a small background risk of someone breaking the record at early national trials or a record being retroactively annulled due to doping (though his record is over 2 years old, making this less probable immediately). Consequently, confidence that he retains the record is very high in the short term, with uncertainty only slightly increasing for the further resolution date in March 2026.
